web.markdown:
[Jump to content](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#bodyContent) From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Venezuelan airline | [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/15/Avior_Airlines_2017.svg/250px-Avior_Airlines_2017.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Avior_Airlines_2017.svg) | | [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/YV2928_%2815063877737%29.jpg/250px-YV2928_%2815063877737%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:YV2928_(15063877737).jpg) | | | [IATA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airline_codes#IATA_airline_designator "Airline codes") | [ICAO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airline_codes#ICAO_airline_designator "Airline codes") | [Call sign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aviation_call_signs#Commercial_airline "Aviation call signs") | | --- | --- | --- | | 9V | ROI | AVIOR | | | Founded | September 3, 1994 | | Commenced operations | June 30, 1995 | | [Hubs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airline_hub "Airline hub") | [General José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_Jos%C3%A9_Antonio_Anzo%C3%A1tegui_International_Airport "General José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport") | | [Secondary hubs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airline_hub#Primary_and_secondary_hubs "Airline hub") | [Simón Bolívar International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sim%C3%B3n_Bol%C3%ADvar_International_Airport_(Venezuela) "Simón Bolívar International Airport (Venezuela)") | | [Frequent-flyer program](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frequent-flyer_program "Frequent-flyer program") | AviorPlus | | [Subsidiaries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subsidiary "Subsidiary") | Pariana de Aviación | | Fleet size | 10 | | Destinations | 13 | | Headquarters | [Barcelona, Venezuela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barcelona,_Venezuela "Barcelona, Venezuela") | | Key people | - Jorge Añez Dager ( [CEO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CEO "CEO") and founder)
- Jose Sulbaran (President) | | Employees | +1,800 (2020) | | Website | [www.aviorair.com](http://www.aviorair.com/) | Avior Airlines **Avior Airlines C.A.** (legally _Aviones de Oriente C.A._) is an [airline](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airline "Airline") based in [Barcelona, Venezuela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barcelona,_Venezuela "Barcelona, Venezuela"). It operates scheduled and charter services within Venezuela and the southern [Caribbean](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caribbean "Caribbean") out of its main hub at [Generál José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gener%C3%A1l_Jos%C3%A9_Antonio_Anzo%C3%A1tegui_International_Airport "Generál José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport").[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-FI-1) It is currently the largest private capital airline in Venezuela in terms of fleet, destinations and its more than 1,800 employees nationally and internationally. ## History \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=1 "Edit section: History")\] Founded by Jorge Luis Añez Dager and Rafael Ciarcia Walo,[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-2) the airline was established as **Avior Express**, and started operations on June 30, 1995, initially using a single five-seat [Cessna Skymaster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cessna_Skymaster "Cessna Skymaster") for charter flights to [Margarita Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Margarita_Island "Margarita Island") and [Canaima](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canaima_National_Park "Canaima National Park"). It is fully owned by Jorge Añez Dager.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-FI-1) In 2009, Avior Airlines entered a temporary bankruptcy crisis, leading to the suspension of most of its routes as well as the sale of all of their 12 [Beechcraft 1900D](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beechcraft_1900D "Beechcraft 1900D"). For 2012, Avior Airlines announced the creation of a new subsidiary, [Avior Regional](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Regional "Avior Regional"), that would cover the old routes suspended in 2009, as well as the purchase of 4 [Boeing 737-400s](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-400 "Boeing 737-400") for international flights. In 2013, the first of the 737s arrived, as did the first [Fokker 50](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fokker_50 "Fokker 50") intended for Avior Regional. Due to delays in the process of getting the new airline certified by the [National Institute of Civil Aviation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Institute_of_Civil_Aviation "National Institute of Civil Aviation"), the Fokker 50s were employed by the parent company until mid-2015, when they were transferred to Avior Regional's fleet. In 2015, Avior started an ambitious process of expanding its fleet and destinations, intending to encourage Venezuelan air connectivity, as a result of the reduction of flights from foreign airlines to the country. On December 3, 2017, Avior was added to the [list of airlines banned in the European Union](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_airlines_banned_in_the_European_Union "List of airlines banned in the European Union"), due to failing to meet the EU's safety requirements.[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-3)[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-4) ## Destinations \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=2 "Edit section: Destinations")\] As of November 2025, Avior Airlines flies to the following destinations:[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-5)[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-6) | Country | City | Airport | Notes | Refs | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Flag_of_Colombia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Colombia.svg.png)[Colombia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") | [Bogotá](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá") | [El Dorado International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Dorado_International_Airport "El Dorado International Airport") | | [\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-7) | | [Medellín](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Medell%C3%ADn "Medellín") | [José María Córdova International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Mar%C3%ADa_C%C3%B3rdova_International_Airport "José María Córdova International Airport") | | [\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-8) | | ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Flag_of_Cura%C3%A7ao.svg/40px-Flag_of_Cura%C3%A7ao.svg.png)[Curaçao](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cura%C3%A7ao "Curaçao") | [Willemstad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Willemstad "Willemstad") | [Curaçao International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cura%C3%A7ao_International_Airport "Curaçao International Airport") | | [\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-9) | | ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/06/Flag_of_Venezuela.svg/40px-Flag_of_Venezuela.svg.png)[Venezuela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") | | [Barcelona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barcelona,_Venezuela "Barcelona, Venezuela") | [General José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_Jos%C3%A9_Antonio_Anzo%C3%A1tegui_International_Airport "General José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport") | [Hub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airline_hub "Airline hub") | | | [Barquisimeto](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barquisimeto "Barquisimeto") | [Jacinto Lara International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jacinto_Lara_International_Airport "Jacinto Lara International Airport") | | | | [Caracas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caracas "Caracas") | [Simón Bolívar International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sim%C3%B3n_Bol%C3%ADvar_International_Airport_(Venezuela) "Simón Bolívar International Airport (Venezuela)") | [Hub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airline_hub "Airline hub") | | | [El Vigia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Vigia "El Vigia") | [Juan Pablo Pérez Alfonzo Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Juan_Pablo_P%C3%A9rez_Alfonzo_Airport "Juan Pablo Pérez Alfonzo Airport") | | | | [Las Piedras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Las_Piedras,_Venezuela "Las Piedras, Venezuela") | [Josefa Camejo International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Josefa_Camejo_International_Airport "Josefa Camejo International Airport") | | [\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-10) | | [Maracaibo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maracaibo "Maracaibo") | [La Chinita International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/La_Chinita_International_Airport "La Chinita International Airport") | | | | [Puerto Ayacucho](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puerto_Ayacucho "Puerto Ayacucho") | [Cacique Aramare Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cacique_Aramare_Airport "Cacique Aramare Airport") | | [\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-11) | | [Puerto Ordaz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puerto_Ordaz "Puerto Ordaz") | [Manuel Carlos Piar Guayana Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manuel_Carlos_Piar_Guayana_Airport "Manuel Carlos Piar Guayana Airport") | | [\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-12) | ## Fleet \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=3 "Edit section: Fleet")\] ### Current \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=4 "Edit section: Current")\] [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/05/YV2928_%2815125208008%29.jpg/250px-YV2928_%2815125208008%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:YV2928_(15125208008).jpg) An Avior [Boeing 737-400](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-400 "Boeing 737-400") landing at [Miami International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miami_International_Airport "Miami International Airport") in 2014 As of November 2025[\[update\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit), the Avior Airlines fleet includes the following aircraft:[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-13) | Aircraft | In
service | Orders | Passengers | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | C | Y | Total | | [Boeing 737-200](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-200 "Boeing 737-200") | 2 | — | 12 | 96 | 108 | | | [Boeing 737-400](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-400 "Boeing 737-400") | 8 | — | 12 | 132 | 144 | | | 138 | 150 | | Total | 10 | — | | Avior Airlines fleet ### Former \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=5 "Edit section: Former")\] [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c6/152as_-_Avior_Beech_1900D_Airliner%3B_YV-503C%40PMV%3B10.10.2001_%284794606030%29.jpg/250px-152as_-_Avior_Beech_1900D_Airliner%3B_YV-503C%40PMV%3B10.10.2001_%284794606030%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:152as_-_Avior_Beech_1900D_Airliner;_YV-503C@PMV;10.10.2001_(4794606030).jpg) A former Avior [Beechcraft 1900D](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beechcraft_1900D "Beechcraft 1900D") parked at [Santiago Mariño Caribbean International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Santiago_Mari%C3%B1o_Caribbean_International_Airport "Santiago Mariño Caribbean International Airport") in 2001 Avior Airlines formerly operated the following aircraft: | Aircraft | Total | Introduced | Retired | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | [Aero Commander 500](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aero_Commander_500 "Aero Commander 500") | 1 | 1994 | Unknown | | | [Airbus A340-300](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbus_A340-300 "Airbus A340-300") | 1 | 2016 | 2019 | Transferred to [Conviasa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conviasa "Conviasa") | | [Beechcraft 1900C](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beechcraft_1900C "Beechcraft 1900C") | 2 | 1997 | 1999 | | | [Beechcraft 1900D](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beechcraft_1900D "Beechcraft 1900D") | 12 | 1997 | 2010 | | | [Cessna 208B Grand Caravan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cessna_208B_Grand_Caravan "Cessna 208B Grand Caravan") | 6 | 1998 | 2006 | | | [Cessna Skymaster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cessna_Skymaster "Cessna Skymaster") | 1 | 1994 | Unknown | | | [Dornier Do 28](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dornier_Do_28 "Dornier Do 28") | 3 | 1995 | 1999 | | | [Embraer EMB 120ER Brasilia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Embraer_EMB_120ER_Brasilia "Embraer EMB 120ER Brasilia") | 3 | 2001 | 2007 | One leased from [Avensa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avensa "Avensa") | | [Fokker 50](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fokker_50 "Fokker 50") | 6 | 2012 | 2015 | Transferred to [Avior Regional](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Regional "Avior Regional") | ## Accidents and incidents \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=6 "Edit section: Accidents and incidents")\] - On July 15, 1998, a [Beechcraft 1900D](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beechcraft_1900D "Beechcraft 1900D") was hijacked by four armed masked hijackers during a domestic flight. The hijackers were among 22 people on board the aircraft, which was flying from [Caracas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caracas "Caracas") to [Barinas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barinas,_Barinas "Barinas, Barinas"). The hijackers forced the plane to divert to a remote airstrip at a cattle ranch. The hijackers released the passengers and crew and took the plane to [Colombia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"), where it was later recovered.[\[14\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-14) - On July 30, 1999, another [Beechcraft 1900D](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beechcraft_1900D "Beechcraft 1900D") (registered YV-466C) was hijacked, this time by three men and two women. The aircraft was flying from [Caracas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caracas "Caracas") to [Guasdualito](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guasdualito "Guasdualito"), via [Barinas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barinas,_Barinas "Barinas, Barinas"). Coordinates were given to the flight crew and landed the plane in [Arauca, Colombia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arauca,_Arauca "Arauca, Arauca").[\[15\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-15) - On January 7, 2009, a [Boeing 737-200](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-200 "Boeing 737-200") (registered YV1360), flying from [Oranjestad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oranjestad,_Aruba "Oranjestad, Aruba") to [Valencia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valencia,_Carabobo "Valencia, Carabobo"), made an emergency landing after an engine fire was reported. The aircraft diverted to [Simón Bolívar International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sim%C3%B3n_Bol%C3%ADvar_International_Airport_(Venezuela) "Simón Bolívar International Airport (Venezuela)") where the aircraft landed safely.[\[16\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-16) All 81 occupants on board were uninjured. - On October 31, 2014, a [Boeing 737-400](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-400 "Boeing 737-400") (registered YV2946) suffered a tire burst during takeoff at [General José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_Jos%C3%A9_Antonio_Anzo%C3%A1tegui_International_Airport "General José Antonio Anzoátegui International Airport").[\[17\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-17) The flight crew aborted the takeoff and the aircraft stopped with a burst and deflated tire on the left-hand main landing gear. None of the 144 occupants on board were injured. - On March 3, 2018, a [Boeing 737-400](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-400 "Boeing 737-400") flying from [Barcelona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona") to [Guayaquil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guayaquil "Guayaquil") ran off the runway during landing at [José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Joaqu%C3%ADn_de_Olmedo_International_Airport "José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport"). No injuries were reported, and the aircraft suffered no structural damage. The causes of this inconvenience were mainly due to the wet track and the heavy rain that fell in the city. - On November 22, 2019, a [Boeing 737-400](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-400 "Boeing 737-400") (registered YV3012) was servicing a flight between [Valencia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valencia "Valencia") and [Bogotá](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá"). Upon landing at [El Dorado International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Dorado_International_Airport "El Dorado International Airport"), its right main landing gear suffered a serious malfunction, causing it to collapse, which led to an evacuation once the aircraft came to a halt.[\[18\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-18)[\[19\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-19) - On December 6, 2019, a [Boeing 737-400](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_737-400 "Boeing 737-400") (registered YV3011) took off at 8AM flying from [Lima](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lima "Lima") to [Caracas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caracas "Caracas") when it suffered a depressurization 45 minutes after takeoff.[\[20\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_note-20) There were 133 passengers and 8 crew members on board. Some passengers suffered a lack of oxygen, including a six-month-old child. The aircraft had to make a sharp descent landing emergency in the city of [Tarapoto](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tarapoto "Tarapoto"), Peru. The airline enabled an aircraft to comply with the scheduled itinerary. ## See also \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=7 "Edit section: See also")\] - [List of airlines of Venezuela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_airlines_of_Venezuela "List of airlines of Venezuela") - [List of airlines banned in the European Union](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_airlines_banned_in_the_European_Union "List of airlines banned in the European Union") ## References \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Avior_Airlines&action=edit§ion=8 "Edit section: References")\] 01. ^ [Jump up to: _**a**_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_ref-FI_1-0) [_**b**_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_ref-FI_1-1)"Directory: World Airlines". _[Flight International](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flight_International "Flight International")_. 2007-03-27. pp. 82–83. 02. **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avior_Airlines#cite_ref-2 "Jump up")**["Vuelos y opiniones sobre Avior Airlines"](https://www.europelowcost.es/companias-aereas/avior-airlines.aspx). web.markdown:
# Venezuelan airline Avior seeks return to US after five-year ban

[ByStephen Pope](https://www.aerotime.aero/articles/author/stephen-pope "View all posts by Stephen Pope")

February 10, 2026

[Airlines](https://www.aerotime.aero/category/airlines "Airlines")![avior](https://www.aerotime.aero/images/2026/02/avior-800x500.jpg)
Wikimedia Commons

Venezuelan airline Avior Airlines has asked US regulators for approval to resume flights to the United States.

The move follows a February 3, 2026, filing with the US Department of Transportation in which Avior asked for approval to resume previously approved US service and to add flights to Miami and Houston. The carrier is seeking a two-year authorization.

Some US authority already exists. Avior has long held approval for scheduled service between Barcelona, Venezuela, and Miami, as well as charter authority between the two countries.

The filing follows the Trump administration’s January 29 decision to end a five-year suspension of US-Venezuela air service.

Avior also asked regulators to act on a request it first filed in 2016 to resume service between Caracas and Miami. The department never ruled on that application. In its latest filing, the carrier is asking the Transportation Department to finally act on the request along with its current application.

Avior emphasized that it is a privately owned Venezuelan company with no financial ties to the Venezuelan government and said it remains owned and controlled by Venezuelan nationals. The carrier said it has previously been found financially fit to operate service to the United States and submitted updated financial information under seal in support of its request.

The filing comes as American Airlines recently [moved to restore service to Caracas](https://www.aerotime.aero/articles/faa-reopens-venezuelan-airspace-trump-order-american-airlines "") following the reopening of airspace.

Avior said it is ready to resume flights once approvals are granted and Venezuela’s safety rating is restored, following a US downgrade in 2019.

Avior’s fleet consists of Boeing 737-400 series aircraft, all Venezuelan-registered and owned by the airline. The carrier operates primarily within Venezuela and on some routes to Colombia.

The transportation Department has not yet responded to Avior’s application.

- [Avior Airlines](https://www.aerotime.aero/tag/avior-airlines)
- [United States](https://www.aerotime.aero/tag/united-states)
- [Venezuela](https://www.aerotime.aero/tag/venezuela)
